# Bloqr

> Bloqr is DNS-level ad and tracker blocking with AI-powered filter lists and encrypted DNS (DoH/DoT), protecting every device on a network without installing per-device software.

Bloqr sits at the network's DNS layer: it blocks ads, trackers, and malicious domains before they load, encrypts DNS queries end-to-end, and uses AI to continuously generate and update filter lists as new tracking and phishing domains appear. It works with any device that can point to a custom DNS resolver, and also supports bring-your-own-DNS-provider setups.

## Product

- [Home](https://bloqr.dev/): overview, pricing tiers, and how DNS-level filtering works
- [Pricing](https://bloqr.dev/pricing): plan comparison for personal, family, and team use
- [About](https://bloqr.dev/about): the Bloqr AI project and team
- [Changelog](https://bloqr.dev/changelog): recent product updates

## Learn

- [Why not a private DNS resolver?](https://bloqr.dev/why-not-private): tradeoffs of running your own resolver vs. a managed encrypted DNS service
- [VPN myths](https://bloqr.dev/vpn-myths): how DNS-level blocking differs from and complements a VPN
- [Blog](https://bloqr.dev/blog): posts on DNS privacy, ad-tech tracking, and phishing infrastructure

- [Privacy Policy](https://bloqr.dev/privacy)
